name: run-cmd
description: Run arbitrary commands in a specified directory with optional environment variables, timeout, output limiting via --head/--tail, stack trace/ANSI trimming, or run Python code inline with --py (auto-activates venv), replacing cd+command, timeout wrapper, python3 heredoc, and pipe-to-head/tail patterns.

parameters.name: --head=N
parameters.description: Show only first N lines of output

parameters.name: --tail=N
parameters.description: Show only last N lines of output

parameters.name: --trim-stack
parameters.description: Strip stack trace lines from output, keeping only error messages and code lines

parameters.name: --trim-ansi
parameters.description: Strip ANSI escape codes from output (reduces observation size)

parameters.name: --py, --python
parameters.description: Run Python code inline (next arg as code, or read from stdin); auto-activates .venv/venv/env if found

cost_saving_rationale: Collapses cd + export + timeout + command into one step, and optionally strips ANSI codes/stack traces or limits output lines to reduce observation size; --head/--tail replace cmd | head/tail two-step pattern; --py replaces python3 heredoc (cd + python3 << PYINPUT) pattern, saving 1+ step per use.
